data.narrative:
Hermeus is a Series C stage hypersonic and supersonic technology company founded by AJ Piplica, Skyler Shuford, Glenn Case, and Mike Smayda. The company currently carries a $1.0B post-money valuation and has raised $350M to date. The broader Supersonic & Hypersonic sector comprises a limited peer universe: two public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and one private peer (Boom Supersonic), reflecting the nascent and capital-intensive nature of this frontier segment.

Given the sparse peer set within the Supersonic & Hypersonic category, a quantitative peer-median approach was not viable; a broader sectoral median would introduce material noise. Instead, valuation relies on stage-progression methodology applied to Hermeus's last post-money valuation. Using a typical Series C to next-stage step-up multiplier of 1.7x applied to the $1.0B base yields an implied valuation of $1.7B. This method assumes progression consistent with venture capital's historical step-up cadence for frontier-tech companies advancing through growth stages.

The triangulated valuation range is $1.4B (low) to $2.0B (high), with $1.7B serving as the median point. Key uncertainties center on the absence of narrow-peer validation—Hermeus operates in a constrained competitive universe with minimal public market comparables and only one identified private peer, limiting cross-validation of stage-based assumptions. Technology execution risk and regulatory pathway clarity for hypersonic platforms represent material valuation drivers not fully captured in quantitative analysis alone.
